I wondered if it was only me, because I’m obviously not the most objective observer in the world when it comes to Trump, so afterward I flipped on Fox News for a few minutes, long enough to see that even Sean Hannity couldn’t rouse himself into a state of excitation.

sentences.en: First of all, I could barely stay awake.
sentences.zh: 首先，我几乎无法保持清醒。

sentences.en: It was only a 25-minute speech, and it’s not like 9:25 p.m.
sentences.zh: 这只是一个 25 分钟的演讲，而且不是晚上 9 点 25 分。

sentences.en: is my bedtime, but President Donald Trump’s affect was so strained, his rhetoric so ploddingly predictable, and his “evidence” so vaporous that my mind started wandering at around 9:13.
sentences.zh: 是我的就寝时间，但唐纳德·特朗普总统的情绪如此紧张，他的言论如此乏味可预测，而他的“证据”又如此模糊，以至于我的思绪在 9 点 13 分左右开始走神。

sentences.en: I wondered if it was only me, because I’m obviously not the most objective observer in the world when it comes to Trump, so afterward I flipped on Fox News for a few minutes, long enough to see that even Sean Hannity couldn’t rouse himself into a state of excitation.
sentences.zh: 我想知道是不是只有我一个人这么认为，因为对于特朗普来说，我显然不是世界上最客观的观察者，所以后来我翻看了几分钟福克斯新闻，足以看到连肖恩·汉尼提都无法让自己兴奋起来。

blocks.html_en: So Trump’s much-ballyhooed (chiefly by him) speech Thursday night was a dud. So say I; so, in not so many words, said Hannity (perhaps lurking in the corners of his mind last night was the nearly $800 million his network paid out in a legal settlement after it was caught promoting Trump’s lies); so said Playbook this morning (“Trump’s anticlimax”); so said the gang on Morning Joe. That’s good. It tells us that that power he had over people and institutions a year or 15 months ago has evanesced.

sentences.en: So Trump’s much-ballyhooed (chiefly by him) speech Thursday night was a dud.
sentences.zh: 因此，特朗普周四晚上大肆宣扬的（主要是他本人）的演讲是一场失败的演讲。

sentences.en: So say I; so, in not so many words, said Hannity (perhaps lurking in the corners of his mind last night was the nearly $800 million his network paid out in a legal settlement after it was caught promoting Trump’s lies); so said Playbook this morning (“Trump’s anticlimax”); so said the gang on Morning Joe.
sentences.zh: 我这么说；汉尼蒂说，话不多说（也许昨晚潜伏在他脑海角落的是他的网络在被发现宣扬特朗普的谎言后通过法律和解支付了近 8 亿美元）； Playbook 今天早上如是说（“特朗普的虎头蛇尾”）； 《早安乔》的一伙人如是说。

sentences.en: That’s good.
sentences.zh: 那挺好的。

sentences.en: It tells us that that power he had over people and institutions a year or 15 months ago has evanesced.
sentences.zh: 它告诉我们，一年或 15 个月前他对人和机构的权力已经消失。

blocks.html_en: More than that, it was just an embarrassing moment for the United States of America. When has a president ever done anything like that—commandeered the bully pulpit to spread poisonous half-truths and lies? Presidents often lie about this or that particular thing. But no one has ever pressed an obviously bogus case like that. Even the throwaway lines were poisonous lies (“We had transgender for everybody” before he returned to the White House). So it’s unlikely that most Americans are taking it very seriously.

sentences.en: More than that, it was just an embarrassing moment for the United States of America.
sentences.zh: 更重要的是，这对美利坚合众国来说是一个尴尬的时刻。

sentences.en: When has a president ever done anything like that—commandeered the bully pulpit to spread poisonous half-truths and lies?
sentences.zh: 总统什么时候做过这样的事——霸占讲坛传播有毒的半真半假和谎言？

sentences.en: Presidents often lie about this or that particular thing.
sentences.zh: 总统经常在这件事或那件事上撒谎。

sentences.en: But no one has ever pressed an obviously bogus case like that.
sentences.zh: 但从来没有人对这样一个明显是伪造的案件进行过调查。

sentences.en: Even the throwaway lines were poisonous lies (“We had transgender for everybody” before he returned to the White House).
sentences.zh: 即使是一次性的台词也是有毒的谎言（在他返回白宫之前，“我们每个人都有变性人”）。

sentences.en: So it’s unlikely that most Americans are taking it very seriously.
sentences.zh: 因此，大多数美国人不太可能认真对待它。

blocks.html_en: Still: He’s the president, he’s backed by a couple hundred lapdogs on Capitol Hill who will repeat and endorse whatever he says, and of course he is reinforced by a propaganda network that reaches millions of Americans every day that will do the same. So the speech still matters, and it may set in train naked attempts to rig the election and prevent some Americans from voting.

sentences.en: Still: He’s the president, he’s backed by a couple hundred lapdogs on Capitol Hill who will repeat and endorse whatever he says, and of course he is reinforced by a propaganda network that reaches millions of Americans every day that will do the same.
sentences.zh: 尽管如此：他是总统，他得到了国会山几百名哈巴狗的支持，他们会重复并支持他所说的一切，当然，他还得到了每天影响数百万美国人的宣传网络的强化，他们也会做同样的事情。

sentences.en: So the speech still matters, and it may set in train naked attempts to rig the election and prevent some Americans from voting.
sentences.zh: 因此，这篇演讲仍然很重要，它可能会赤裸裸地企图操纵选举并阻止一些美国人投票。

blocks.html_en: But having seen the speech, and despite what everyone is saying, and indeed despite what I myself assumed going in, I’m not so sure that the main point here was to lay some kind of groundwork for November. Trump may be the most corrupt and furtive man ever to occupy the presidency, but in this one way he is achingly transparent: His motivations are always right out there. And his motivation last night wasn’t really November. It was just what he said. It was 2020.

sentences.en: But having seen the speech, and despite what everyone is saying, and indeed despite what I myself assumed going in, I’m not so sure that the main point here was to lay some kind of groundwork for November.
sentences.zh: 但看过这篇演讲后，尽管每个人都在说什么，而且事实上，尽管我自己假设要演讲，但我不太确定这里的要点是否是为 11 月奠定某种基础。

sentences.en: Trump may be the most corrupt and furtive man ever to occupy the presidency, but in this one way he is achingly transparent: His motivations are always right out there.
sentences.zh: 特朗普可能是有史以来最腐败、最鬼祟的总统，但在这一点上，他是极其透明的：他的动机总是显而易见的。

sentences.en: And his motivation last night wasn’t really November.
sentences.zh: 而他昨晚的动机并不是真正的十一月。

sentences.en: It was just what he said.
sentences.zh: 这就是他所说的。

sentences.en: It was 2020.

blocks.html_en: This man-child with the emotional life of an 11-year-old and an ego that bruises more easily than a three-day-old banana will never stop being obsessed about 2020. In Trump’s mind, Trump can’t lose. It simply can’t happen. (Privately, he has admitted to more than one former aide that he lost, but let’s set that aside for now.) And nothing is ever his fault. Ergo, the election had to be stolen. And he has to “prove” it.

sentences.en: This man-child with the emotional life of an 11-year-old and an ego that bruises more easily than a three-day-old banana will never stop being obsessed about 2020.
sentences.zh: 这个有着 11 岁孩子般情感生活和比三天大香蕉更容易受伤的自尊心的男孩永远不会停止对 2020 年的痴迷。

sentences.en: In Trump’s mind, Trump can’t lose.
sentences.zh: 在特朗普看来，特朗普不能输。

sentences.en: It simply can’t happen.
sentences.zh: 这根本不可能发生。

sentences.en: (Privately, he has admitted to more than one former aide that he lost, but let’s set that aside for now.)
sentences.zh: （私下里，他已经向不止一位前助手承认他失去了，但我们现在先把这件事放在一边。）

sentences.en: And nothing is ever his fault.
sentences.zh: 没有什么是他的错。

sentences.en: Ergo, the election had to be stolen.
sentences.zh: 因此，选举必须被窃取。

sentences.en: And he has to “prove” it.
sentences.zh: 他必须“证明”这一点。

blocks.html_en: A normal person would let this go after a while and start focusing on the future. The broad interpretation that last night’s speech was really about 2026 and 2028 is a function of people making that assumption. But it’s a fatal assumption in this case. Trump is not normal. Trump is possessed.

sentences.en: A normal person would let this go after a while and start focusing on the future.
sentences.zh: 正常人会在一段时间后放下这件事并开始关注未来。

sentences.en: The broad interpretation that last night’s speech was really about 2026 and 2028 is a function of people making that assumption.
sentences.zh: 昨晚的演讲实际上是关于 2026 年和 2028 年的广泛解释是人们做出这一假设的结果。

sentences.en: But it’s a fatal assumption in this case.
sentences.zh: 但在这种情况下，这是一个致命的假设。

sentences.en: Trump is not normal.
sentences.zh: 特朗普不正常。

sentences.en: Trump is possessed.
sentences.zh: 特朗普着魔了。

blocks.html_en: His loss must be vindicated, whatever it takes. It might be vindicated by lies. He will know deep down that they are lies, but he won’t care. For Trump, the world is a jungle—eat or be eaten. It’s an authoritarian worldview, because if that’s what you believe, then any action is justified to avoid being the eaten. But he can’t drop it until the vindication is received.

sentences.en: His loss must be vindicated, whatever it takes.
sentences.zh: 无论如何，他的损失必须得到平反。

sentences.en: It might be vindicated by lies.
sentences.zh: 或许可以通过谎言来证明这一点。

sentences.en: He will know deep down that they are lies, but he won’t care.
sentences.zh: 他内心深处知道这些都是谎言，但他不在乎。

sentences.en: For Trump, the world is a jungle—eat or be eaten.
sentences.zh: 对于特朗普来说，世界就是一个丛林——要么吃，要么被吃。

sentences.en: It’s an authoritarian worldview, because if that’s what you believe, then any action is justified to avoid being the eaten.
sentences.zh: 这是一种独裁的世界观，因为如果你相信这一点，那么为避免被吃掉而采取的任何行动都是合理的。

sentences.en: But he can’t drop it until the vindication is received.
sentences.zh: 但在得到平反之前他不能放弃它。

blocks.html_en: Plenty of dangers lurk for the republic, despite his motivation being mainly retroactive, by the way. The sycophant he wants to install as attorney general, who referred to himself as Trump’s lawyer at his nomination hearing this week, has proven himself more than capable of filing charges against anyone Trump wants him to indict. And the sycophant he wants to install as director of national intelligence, who at his hearing this week couldn’t bring himself to say that Joe Biden won the 2020 election, will presumably gather intel on anyone Trump tells him to look into. So we have every reason on earth to fear that if Todd Blanche and Jay Clayton are confirmed in their respective positions, they’ll take a torch to the Constitution in an attempt to give Trump his vindication.

sentences.en: Plenty of dangers lurk for the republic, despite his motivation being mainly retroactive, by the way.
sentences.zh: 顺便说一句，尽管他的动机主要是追溯性的，但共和国潜藏着许多危险。

sentences.en: The sycophant he wants to install as attorney general, who referred to himself as Trump’s lawyer at his nomination hearing this week, has proven himself more than capable of filing charges against anyone Trump wants him to indict.
sentences.zh: 他想任命的这位阿谀奉承者为司法部长，在本周的提名听证会上自称为特朗普的律师，他已经证明自己完全有能力对特朗普希望他起诉的任何人提出指控。

sentences.en: And the sycophant he wants to install as director of national intelligence, who at his hearing this week couldn’t bring himself to say that Joe Biden won the 2020 election, will presumably gather intel on anyone Trump tells him to look into.
sentences.zh: 他想要任命的国家情报总监的谄媚者，在本周的听证会上无法让自己说乔·拜登赢得了 2020 年大选，他可能会收集特朗普要求他调查的任何人的情报。

sentences.en: So we have every reason on earth to fear that if Todd Blanche and Jay Clayton are confirmed in their respective positions, they’ll take a torch to the Constitution in an attempt to give Trump his vindication.
sentences.zh: 因此，我们完全有理由担心，如果托德·布兰奇和杰伊·克莱顿的职务得到确认，他们将举起宪法火炬，试图为特朗普辩护。

blocks.html_en: So picture it. Two and a half more years of sham indictments, destroyed reputations, phony charges repeated by state propagandists who call themselves journalists. And since none of it will work, because the 2020 election was not in fact stolen, it will continue until the day Trump leaves office. Beyond, actually. He’ll be on this jihad until the day that last well-done steak with ketchup arrests whatever’s left of his heart.

sentences.en: So picture it.
sentences.zh: 想象一下吧。

sentences.en: Two and a half more years of sham indictments, destroyed reputations, phony charges repeated by state propagandists who call themselves journalists.
sentences.zh: 两年半以来，自称记者的国家宣传人员不断提出虚假起诉、声誉受损和虚假指控。

sentences.en: And since none of it will work, because the 2020 election was not in fact stolen, it will continue until the day Trump leaves office.
sentences.zh: 而且既然这一切都行不通，因为2020年的选举实际上并没有被窃取，所以它将持续到特朗普卸任的那一天。

sentences.en: Beyond, actually.
sentences.zh: 实际上，超越了。

sentences.en: He’ll be on this jihad until the day that last well-done steak with ketchup arrests whatever’s left of his heart.
sentences.zh: 他将参加这场圣战，直到最后一块全熟的番茄酱牛排逮捕他剩下的心的那一天。

blocks.html_en: That’s bad enough. But yes, inevitably, the speech will have ramifications for this November. It’s hard to know right now what they will be. On the one hand, it seems that his cherished SAVE America Act will never become law. So he probably can’t do that.

sentences.en: That’s bad enough.
sentences.zh: 这已经够糟糕了。

sentences.en: But yes, inevitably, the speech will have ramifications for this November.
sentences.zh: 但是，是的，这次演讲不可避免地会对今年 11 月产生影响。

sentences.en: It’s hard to know right now what they will be.
sentences.zh: 现在很难知道它们会是什么。

sentences.en: On the one hand, it seems that his cherished SAVE America Act will never become law.
sentences.zh: 一方面，他所珍视的《拯救美国法案》似乎永远不会成为法律。

sentences.en: So he probably can’t do that.
sentences.zh: 所以他可能做不到。

blocks.html_en: Otherwise, though, saying what he said last night about 2020 tees it up for him to repeat as needed between now and November 3 that he—and Attorney General Blanche and DNI Clayton—have unearthed new evidence that the deep staters are up to their old 2020 tricks. Therefore, they have no choice but to deploy ICE agents to certain polling places. This is clearly against federal law. Blanche had said at the Conservative Political Action Conference in March that such a move might be necessary; at his hearing, under questioning from Senator Amy Klobuchar, he said he’d “follow the law,” but his language was slippery.

sentences.en: Otherwise, though, saying what he said last night about 2020 tees it up for him to repeat as needed between now and November 3 that he—and Attorney General Blanche and DNI Clayton—have unearthed new evidence that the deep staters are up to their old 2020 tricks.
sentences.zh: 不过，除此之外，他昨晚所说的有关 2020 年的言论也为他在从现在到 11 月 3 日之间根据需要重复的话做好了准备，他和司法部长布兰奇和国家情报总监克莱顿已经发现了新的证据，表明深层国家者正在实施他们的 2020 年老把戏。

sentences.en: Therefore, they have no choice but to deploy ICE agents to certain polling places.
sentences.zh: 因此，他们别无选择，只能在某些投票站部署ICE特工。

sentences.en: This is clearly against federal law.
sentences.zh: 这显然违反联邦法律。

sentences.en: Blanche had said at the Conservative Political Action Conference in March that such a move might be necessary; at his hearing, under questioning from Senator Amy Klobuchar, he said he’d “follow the law,” but his language was slippery.
sentences.zh: 布兰奇在三月份的保守党政治行动会议上表示，此举可能是必要的；在听证会上，在参议员艾米·克洛布彻 (Amy Klobuchar) 的质询下，他表示自己会“遵守法律”，但他的语言很狡猾。

blocks.html_en: In sum: Trump’s motivation may be retroactive, but for the Republican Party and the right-wing media machine, the speech’s greatest utility is proactive. Either way, we’re left with the grim reality that a steaming pile of lies that nearly everyone knows to be lies could end up exerting a chilling influence over the administration of justice in this country and our electoral process.

sentences.en: In sum: Trump’s motivation may be retroactive, but for the Republican Party and the right-wing media machine, the speech’s greatest utility is proactive.
sentences.zh: 总而言之：特朗普的动机可能是追溯性的，但对于共和党和右翼媒体机器来说，这次演讲的最大效用是主动性。

sentences.en: Either way, we’re left with the grim reality that a steaming pile of lies that nearly everyone knows to be lies could end up exerting a chilling influence over the administration of justice in this country and our electoral process.
sentences.zh: 无论哪种方式，我们都面临着严峻的现实，即几乎每个人都知道是谎言的一大堆谎言最终可能会对这个国家的司法和我们的选举进程产生令人不寒而栗的影响。

blocks.html_en: At the same time, let’s conclude on a more positive note. The reaction to the speech is for the most part encouragingly negative and dismissive. The New York Times, well known for the cowardly equivocation of its homepage headlines regarding such matters, actually used the phrase “outlandish claims” in an early headline. For the Times, that’s a shot of Tabasco sauce.

sentences.en: At the same time, let’s conclude on a more positive note.
sentences.zh: 与此同时，让我们以更积极的态度来总结。

sentences.en: The reaction to the speech is for the most part encouragingly negative and dismissive.
sentences.zh: 对这次演讲的反应大多是令人鼓舞的消极和轻蔑。

sentences.en: The New York Times, well known for the cowardly equivocation of its homepage headlines regarding such matters, actually used the phrase “outlandish claims” in an early headline.
sentences.zh: 《纽约时报》以其主页标题对此类问题的怯懦模棱两可而闻名，实际上在早期的标题中使用了“古怪的主张”一词。

sentences.en: For the Times, that’s a shot of Tabasco sauce.
sentences.zh: 对于《纽约时报》来说，这是一杯塔巴斯科辣酱。

blocks.html_en: Maybe this speech, combined with those Justice Department goons serving subpoenas to four Times reporters at their homes, will finally convince executive editor Joe Kahn that a newspaper’s job actually is to fight for democracy. The water’s fine, Joe; as Victor Laszlo said to Rick Blaine, it’s never too late to join the fight.

sentences.en: Maybe this speech, combined with those Justice Department goons serving subpoenas to four Times reporters at their homes, will finally convince executive editor Joe Kahn that a newspaper’s job actually is to fight for democracy.
sentences.zh: 也许这篇演讲，再加上司法部的暴徒在四名《纽约时报》记者家中发出传票，最终会让执行主编乔·卡恩相信，报纸的工作实际上是为民主而战。

sentences.en: The water’s fine, Joe; as Victor Laszlo said to Rick Blaine, it’s never too late to join the fight.
sentences.zh: 水很好，乔；正如维克多·拉斯洛（Victor Laszlo）对里克·布莱恩（Rick Blaine）所说，加入战斗永远不会太晚。

blocks.html_en: The conventional media wisdom is that Maine GOP Senator Susan Collins is sitting back and laughing this week, and well she might be. The collapse of Graham Platner’s campaign is an implosion for the ages, and it puts the state’s Democrats in a tricky situation they need to navigate skillfully in these next two weeks.

sentences.en: The conventional media wisdom is that Maine GOP Senator Susan Collins is sitting back and laughing this week, and well she might be.
sentences.zh: 传统媒体的看法是，缅因州共和党参议员苏珊·柯林斯本周会坐下来大笑，而且她也可能会这样。

sentences.en: The collapse of Graham Platner’s campaign is an implosion for the ages, and it puts the state’s Democrats in a tricky situation they need to navigate skillfully in these next two weeks.
sentences.zh: 格雷厄姆·普拉特纳(Graham Platner)竞选活动的失败是多年来的一次内爆，这让该州的民主党人陷入了一个棘手的境地，他们需要在接下来的两周内巧妙地应对。

blocks.html_en: But November is a long way away. If—and it’s a big but by no means insurmountable if—the Democrats make it through these next two weeks without too many bruises and unite behind a nominee, Collins is still going to be fighting for her life in a Democratic state where Donald Trump’s approval rating is 36 percent, where 85 percent say the state’s economy is fair or poor, and where the generic Democratic congressional edge in one recent poll is a hefty 11 percent. Those are all terrifying numbers for Collins, and she knows it.

sentences.en: But November is a long way away.
sentences.zh: 但距离十一月还很遥远。

sentences.en: If—and it’s a big but by no means insurmountable if—the Democrats make it through these next two weeks without too many bruises and unite behind a nominee, Collins is still going to be fighting for her life in a Democratic state where Donald Trump’s approval rating is 36 percent, where 85 percent say the state’s economy is fair or poor, and where the generic Democratic congressional edge in one recent poll is a hefty 11 percent.
sentences.zh: 如果——这是一个很大但绝不是不可克服的假设——民主党在接下来的两周内毫发无损地度过了难关，并团结在一名提名人的身后，那么柯林斯仍将在民主党州为自己的生活而奋斗，唐纳德·特朗普的支持率为36%，85%的人认为该州的经济状况良好或较差，而在最近的一项民意调查中，民主党在国会的总体优势为11%。

sentences.en: Those are all terrifying numbers for Collins, and she knows it.
sentences.zh: 这些对于柯林斯来说都是可怕的数字，她知道这一点。

blocks.html_en: Before we get into all that, a few closing thoughts on Platner. I wrote about him a month ago, after The New York Times published some unsavory revelations about his treatment of some former girlfriends (while others said he was fine toward them), and right before the primary. I wrote that since he was almost certain to be the nominee, national Democrats needed to back him.

sentences.en: Before we get into all that, a few closing thoughts on Platner.
sentences.zh: 在我们深入讨论之前，先对普拉特纳做一些总结性的思考。

sentences.en: I wrote about him a month ago, after The New York Times published some unsavory revelations about his treatment of some former girlfriends (while others said he was fine toward them), and right before the primary.
sentences.zh: 我一个月前写了一篇关于他的文章，当时《纽约时报》发表了一些关于他对待一些前女友的令人不快的报道（而其他人则说他对她们很好），就在初选之前。

sentences.en: I wrote that since he was almost certain to be the nominee, national Democrats needed to back him.
sentences.zh: 我写道，由于他几乎肯定会成为提名人，因此全国民主党需要支持他。

blocks.html_en: I did, however, add three caveats, as experience has taught me to do in such situations: “Short of revelations involving murder, rape, or a taste for child pornography, Platner needs to be backed by Democrats to the hilt.” Well, he managed one out of three, but one is enough. It’s utterly and obviously disqualifying.

sentences.en: I did, however, add three caveats, as experience has taught me to do in such situations: “Short of revelations involving murder, rape, or a taste for child pornography, Platner needs to be backed by Democrats to the hilt.”
sentences.zh: 然而，我确实添加了三个警告，正如经验告诉我在这种情况下要做的那样：“如果没有涉及谋杀、强奸或对儿童色情作品的兴趣的揭露，普拉特纳需要得到民主党的全力支持。”

sentences.en: Well, he managed one out of three, but one is enough.
sentences.zh: 好吧，他做到了三分之一，但一个就足够了。

sentences.en: It’s utterly and obviously disqualifying.
sentences.zh: 这显然是完全不合格的。

blocks.html_en: Some Platner defenders have tried to say, What about innocent until proven guilty? That’s ridiculous in the context of running for office. Yes, it means everything in a court of law, where a defendant is on trial for his very freedom; there, he is absolutely entitled to a presumption of innocence, and he must be given his day in court so that we all hear his side. But a political campaign isn’t a criminal trial. In a political campaign, political judgments must be made, and the clear political judgment here is that no party can back someone facing a credible allegation of rape, for God’s sake.

sentences.en: Some Platner defenders have tried to say, What about innocent until proven guilty?
sentences.zh: 一些普拉特纳的捍卫者试图说，在被证明有罪之前，无辜者又如何呢？

sentences.en: That’s ridiculous in the context of running for office.
sentences.zh: 在竞选公职的背景下，这是荒谬的。

sentences.en: Yes, it means everything in a court of law, where a defendant is on trial for his very freedom; there, he is absolutely entitled to a presumption of innocence, and he must be given his day in court so that we all hear his side.
sentences.zh: 是的，这意味着法庭上的一切，被告因其自由而受到审判；在那里，他绝对有权被推定为无罪，而且必须让他出庭，以便我们大家都能听到他的观点。

sentences.en: But a political campaign isn’t a criminal trial.
sentences.zh: 但政治竞选并不是刑事审判。

sentences.en: In a political campaign, political judgments must be made, and the clear political judgment here is that no party can back someone facing a credible allegation of rape, for God’s sake.
sentences.zh: 在政治竞选中，必须做出政治判断，这里明确的政治判断是，看在上帝的份上，任何政党都不能支持面临可信强奸指控的人。

blocks.html_en: As for the intentionally weak vetting of Platner by the young leftist strategists who “discovered” him: I hope people have learned some obvious lessons. Daniel Moraff, the person who’s apparently largely responsible for this debacle, told The Wall Street Journal last month that he sensed a public thirsting for non-cookie-cutter candidates who challenge the status quo. That’s surely true, in a lot of places, but it hardly means you don’t need to put your candidates through the usual paces. It’s grotesquely arrogant and irresponsible, and if Collins ends up winning, Moraff will bear a huge share of the blame.

sentences.en: As for the intentionally weak vetting of Platner by the young leftist strategists who “discovered” him: I hope people have learned some obvious lessons.
sentences.zh: 至于“发现”普拉特纳的年轻左派战略家故意对普拉特纳进行薄弱审查：我希望人们已经吸取了一些明显的教训。

sentences.en: Daniel Moraff, the person who’s apparently largely responsible for this debacle, told The Wall Street Journal last month that he sensed a public thirsting for non-cookie-cutter candidates who challenge the status quo.
sentences.zh: 丹尼尔·莫拉夫（Daniel Moraff）显然对这场灾难负有主要责任，他上个月告诉《华尔街日报》，他感觉到公众渴望挑战现状的非千篇一律的候选人。

sentences.en: That’s surely true, in a lot of places, but it hardly means you don’t need to put your candidates through the usual paces.
sentences.zh: 在很多地方确实如此，但这并不意味着你不需要让你的候选人按照通常的节奏进行。

sentences.en: It’s grotesquely arrogant and irresponsible, and if Collins ends up winning, Moraff will bear a huge share of the blame.
sentences.zh: 这是荒唐的傲慢和不负责任，如果科林斯最终获胜，莫拉夫将承担很大一部分责任。

blocks.html_en: However: I still say, Moraff and Platner aside, Collins could well be in far more trouble with a new Democratic nominee. Kamala Harris beat Trump by seven points in the state. And as I noted above, in a recent poll, respondents said that for Congress, they’d choose a Democrat over a Republican by 53 percent to 42 percent. On top of that, Democratic gubernatorial nominee Hannah Pingree, who will be at the top of the ticket, looks like she’s going to beat her Republican foe by around 15 points. That means Collins is going to have to convince a lot of independents to switch from the D column to the R one as they move down the ballot from governor to senator.

sentences.en: However: I still say, Moraff and Platner aside, Collins could well be in far more trouble with a new Democratic nominee.
sentences.zh: 然而：我仍然要说，除了莫拉夫和普拉特纳之外，柯林斯很可能在新的民主党提名人面前遇到更多麻烦。

sentences.en: Kamala Harris beat Trump by seven points in the state.
sentences.zh: 卡马拉·哈里斯(Kamala Harris)在该州以7个百分点的优势击败特朗普。

sentences.en: And as I noted above, in a recent poll, respondents said that for Congress, they’d choose a Democrat over a Republican by 53 percent to 42 percent.
sentences.zh: 正如我上面提到的，在最近的一项民意调查中，受访者表示，他们在国会中会选择民主党人，而不是共和党人，比例为 53% 对 42%。

sentences.en: On top of that, Democratic gubernatorial nominee Hannah Pingree, who will be at the top of the ticket, looks like she’s going to beat her Republican foe by around 15 points.
sentences.zh: 最重要的是，民主党州长候选人汉娜·平格里(Hannah Pingree)将在竞选中名列前茅，她看起来将以大约15个百分点的优势击败共和党对手。

sentences.en: That means Collins is going to have to convince a lot of independents to switch from the D column to the R one as they move down the ballot from governor to senator.
sentences.zh: 这意味着柯林斯必须说服许多独立人士在从州长到参议员的选票中从民主党转向共和党。

blocks.html_en: Collins has won such voters before, but I would remind people, as they look over past elections for parallels, to take note not only of what’s similar but of what’s different.

sentences.en: Collins has won such voters before, but I would remind people, as they look over past elections for parallels, to take note not only of what’s similar but of what’s different.
sentences.zh: 柯林斯以前曾赢得过这样的选民，但我想提醒人们，在回顾过去的选举中寻找相似之处时，不仅要注意相似之处，还要注意不同之处。

blocks.html_en: The main (as it were) case in point here is 2014. Shenna Bellows, the current secretary of state and a leading contender to replace Platner, was the Democratic nominee against Collins that year. Collins blew her out by 30 points, which some say should raise a lot of red flags.

sentences.en: The main (as it were) case in point here is 2014.
sentences.zh: 这里的主要（可以说）案例是 2014 年。

sentences.en: Shenna Bellows, the current secretary of state and a leading contender to replace Platner, was the Democratic nominee against Collins that year.
sentences.zh: 现任国务卿、接替普拉特纳的主要竞争者申娜·贝洛斯 (Shenna Bellows) 是当年与柯林斯竞争的民主党候选人。

sentences.en: Collins blew her out by 30 points, which some say should raise a lot of red flags.
sentences.zh: 柯林斯以 30 分的优势击败了她，有人说这应该引起很多危险信号。

blocks.html_en: Well, there are a host of differences between 2014 and today. One, Bellows was a 39-year-old novice then, who had never held office and whose claim to fame was that she had been the head of the state’s ACLU. Two, it was the sixth year of a Democratic incumbent presidency, a notoriously hard year for candidates of said party. Three, the Maine of 2014 was in such a cantankerous state that it reelected embarrassing extremist loudmouth Paul LePage as its governor. Four, it was pre-Trump, which changes everything. Five, it was before Collins’s vote to confirm Brett Kavanaugh as a Supreme Court justice because she took him at his word that Roe v. Wade was “settled law.”

sentences.en: Well, there are a host of differences between 2014 and today.
sentences.zh: 嗯，2014 年和今天有很多不同。

sentences.en: One, Bellows was a 39-year-old novice then, who had never held office and whose claim to fame was that she had been the head of the state’s ACLU.
sentences.zh: 第一，贝洛斯当时是一名 39 岁的新手，从未担任过公职，她的成名之处在于她曾担任该州美国公民自由联盟 (ACLU) 的主席。

sentences.en: Two, it was the sixth year of a Democratic incumbent presidency, a notoriously hard year for candidates of said party.
sentences.zh: 第二，今年是民主党现任总统的第六年，对于该党的候选人来说是出了名的艰难的一年。

sentences.en: Three, the Maine of 2014 was in such a cantankerous state that it reelected embarrassing extremist loudmouth Paul LePage as its governor.
sentences.zh: 第三，2014年缅因州的局势如此暴躁，以至于它再次选举令人尴尬的极端主义大嘴保罗·勒佩奇(Paul LePage)担任州长。

sentences.en: Four, it was pre-Trump, which changes everything.
sentences.zh: 第四，特朗普上台之前，一切都改变了。

sentences.en: Five, it was before Collins’s vote to confirm Brett Kavanaugh as a Supreme Court justice because she took him at his word that Roe v.
sentences.zh: 第五，这是在柯林斯投票确认布雷特·卡瓦诺（Brett Kavanaugh）担任最高法院法官之前，因为她相信他的话，即《罗伊诉美国》（Roe v.

sentences.en: Wade was “settled law.”
sentences.zh: 韦德是“既定的法律”。

blocks.html_en: I’m not saying Bellows should be the choice. I don’t know enough about the available candidates or the ins and outs of Maine politics. That’s up to those 600 Democratic delegates to sort out and decide. I’m just saying that what’s past isn’t always prologue. The point is what’s going on now. And what’s going on now, nationally and in Maine, is a strong anti-Trump sentiment—and a GOP incumbent senator who voted to confirm 22 of Trump’s 23 Cabinet nominees.

sentences.en: I’m not saying Bellows should be the choice.
sentences.zh: 我并不是说波纹管应该是选择。

sentences.en: I don’t know enough about the available candidates or the ins and outs of Maine politics.
sentences.zh: 我对可用的候选人或缅因州政治的来龙去脉了解不够。

sentences.en: That’s up to those 600 Democratic delegates to sort out and decide.
sentences.zh: 这要由 600 名民主党代表来整理和决定。

sentences.en: I’m just saying that what’s past isn’t always prologue.
sentences.zh: 我只是说，过去的并不总是序幕。

sentences.en: The point is what’s going on now.
sentences.zh: 重点是现在发生了什么。

sentences.en: And what’s going on now, nationally and in Maine, is a strong anti-Trump sentiment—and a GOP incumbent senator who voted to confirm 22 of Trump’s 23 Cabinet nominees.
sentences.zh: 现在，全国和缅因州都存在强烈的反特朗普情绪，而一位共和党现任参议员投票确认了特朗普 23 名内阁提名人中的 22 名。

blocks.html_en: What the Maine Democratic Party, and to some extent the national Democratic Party, has to do here is run a process that is widely accepted as having two qualities: It must be transparent, and it has to feel fair. The party needs to give voters and citizens regular updates on the process—this is happening today; this is happening next Tuesday—so that everyone feels they’re in the loop and no one feels sandbagged. And it needs to feel fair so that in the end, everyone at least feels that they need to accept the outcome.

sentences.en: What the Maine Democratic Party, and to some extent the national Democratic Party, has to do here is run a process that is widely accepted as having two qualities: It must be transparent, and it has to feel fair.
sentences.zh: 缅因州民主党以及某种程度上的全国民主党在这里必须要做的是运行一个被广泛接受的进程，该进程具有两个品质：它必须是透明的，而且必须让人感觉公平。

sentences.en: The party needs to give voters and citizens regular updates on the process—this is happening today; this is happening next Tuesday—so that everyone feels they’re in the loop and no one feels sandbagged.
sentences.zh: 该党需要定期向选民和公民通报这一进程的最新情况——这就是今天发生的事情；这将在下周二发生，这样每个人都感觉自己处于循环之中，没有人感到被沙袋包围。

sentences.en: And it needs to feel fair so that in the end, everyone at least feels that they need to accept the outcome.
sentences.zh: 而且它需要感觉公平，这样最终每个人至少都觉得他们需要接受结果。

blocks.html_en: As for the national party, I hope Chuck Schumer has the sense to stay completely out of this. His fear of Platner turns out to have been justified, but the way he pushed Governor Janet Mills—who ran as if she didn’t really want to be there—into the race only helped Platner score the nomination. If Schumer and national Democrats are perceived as putting their finger on the scale for a candidate—for instance, in order to stop former state Senate Majority Leader Troy Jackson, who is widely seen as most Platner-like in his populist politics—they’ll only sow rancor and help Collins in the long run.

sentences.en: As for the national party, I hope Chuck Schumer has the sense to stay completely out of this.
sentences.zh: 至于国家党，我希望查克·舒默（Chuck Schumer）有理智，完全不介入此事。

sentences.en: His fear of Platner turns out to have been justified, but the way he pushed Governor Janet Mills—who ran as if she didn’t really want to be there—into the race only helped Platner score the nomination.
sentences.zh: 事实证明，他对普拉特纳的恐惧是有道理的，但他推动州长珍妮特·米尔斯（珍妮特·米尔斯似乎并不想参加竞选）参加竞选的方式只会帮助普拉特纳获得提名。

sentences.en: If Schumer and national Democrats are perceived as putting their finger on the scale for a candidate—for instance, in order to stop former state Senate Majority Leader Troy Jackson, who is widely seen as most Platner-like in his populist politics—they’ll only sow rancor and help Collins in the long run.
sentences.zh: 如果舒默和全国民主党人被认为把矛头指向候选人——例如，为了阻止前州参议院多数党领袖特洛伊·杰克逊（人们普遍认为杰克逊在民粹主义政治方面最像普拉特纳）——从长远来看，他们只会散播仇恨并帮助柯林斯。

blocks.html_en: In the short term, this was a good week for Susan Collins. But to paraphrase Harry Hopkins, people don’t vote in the short term. They vote in November. By then, she could very well be regretting this week’s events.

sentences.en: In the short term, this was a good week for Susan Collins.
sentences.zh: 从短期来看，这对苏珊·柯林斯来说是美好的一周。

sentences.en: But to paraphrase Harry Hopkins, people don’t vote in the short term.
sentences.zh: 但套用哈利·霍普金斯的话来说，人们不会在短期内投票。

sentences.en: They vote in November.
sentences.zh: 他们在十一月投票。

sentences.en: By then, she could very well be regretting this week’s events.
sentences.zh: 到那时，她很可能会对本周的事件感到后悔。

blocks.html_en: History—in this case, through the pen of James Boswell—does not record for us the context in which Samuel Johnson offered up the famous quote that “patriotism is the last refuge of a scoundrel.” According to samueljohnson.com, the English intellectual and polymath just blurted it out on the evening of April 7, 1775, providing no context or explanation of what was on his mind. Some biographers apparently believe he was thinking of William Pitt the Elder, and the former prime minister’s frequent invocation of the term.

sentences.en: History—in this case, through the pen of James Boswell—does not record for us the context in which Samuel Johnson offered up the famous quote that “patriotism is the last refuge of a scoundrel.”
sentences.zh: 历史——在本例中，是通过詹姆斯·博斯韦尔的笔——并没有为我们记录塞缪尔·约翰逊提出“爱国主义是恶棍最后的避难所”这句名言的背景。

sentences.en: According to samueljohnson.com, the English intellectual and polymath just blurted it out on the evening of April 7, 1775, providing no context or explanation of what was on his mind.
sentences.zh: 据 samueljohnson.com 报道，这位英国知识分子和博学家只是在 1775 年 4 月 7 日晚上脱口而出，没有提供任何背景或解释他的想法。

sentences.en: Some biographers apparently believe he was thinking of William Pitt the Elder, and the former prime minister’s frequent invocation of the term.
sentences.zh: 一些传记作者显然相信他想到的是老威廉·皮特，以及这位前首相经常引用的这个词。

blocks.html_en: We do, however, have more thoughts on the matter from Johnson that have survived. The year before, Johnson—something of a mixed bag, politically, but an ardent foe of slavery long before abolitionism became a movement in Great Britain—wrote and delivered to Parliament a speech he called “The Patriot.” It was election time, and Johnson was laying out for the assembled some of his ideas about the duties of public service, and what patriotism does, and does not, mean.

sentences.en: We do, however, have more thoughts on the matter from Johnson that have survived.
sentences.zh: 然而，我们确实有约翰逊对此事的更多想法。

sentences.en: The year before, Johnson—something of a mixed bag, politically, but an ardent foe of slavery long before abolitionism became a movement in Great Britain—wrote and delivered to Parliament a speech he called “The Patriot.”
sentences.zh: 前一年，约翰逊——在政治上是一个复杂的人物，但早在废奴主义在英国成为一场运动之前就已经是奴隶制的热心反对者——撰写并向议会发表了一篇他称之为“爱国者”的演讲。

sentences.en: It was election time, and Johnson was laying out for the assembled some of his ideas about the duties of public service, and what patriotism does, and does not, mean.
sentences.zh: 当时正值选举时间，约翰逊向与会者阐述了他关于公共服务职责以及爱国主义意味着什么和不意味着什么的一些想法。

blocks.html_en: Herewith, just a few choice quotes:

sentences.en: Herewith, just a few choice quotes:
sentences.zh: 谨此，仅摘选几段：

blocks.html_en: “To instigate the populace with rage beyond the provocation, is to suspend publick happiness, if not to destroy it. He is no lover of his country, that unnecessarily disturbs its peace.”

sentences.en: “To instigate the populace with rage beyond the provocation, is to suspend publick happiness, if not to destroy it.
sentences.zh: “以超越挑衅的愤怒煽动民众，即使不是摧毁公众的幸福，也是暂停公众的幸福。

sentences.en: He is no lover of his country, that unnecessarily disturbs its peace.”
sentences.zh: 他不热爱自己的国家，这会不必要地扰乱国家的和平。”

blocks.html_en: “Still less does the true patriot circulate opinions which he knows to be false. No man, who loves his country, fills the nation with clamorous complaints, that the protestant religion is in danger, because ‘popery is established in the extensive province of Quebec,’ a falsehood so open and shameless, that it can need no confutation among those who know that of which it is almost impossible for the most unenlightened zealot to be ignorant.”

sentences.en: “Still less does the true patriot circulate opinions which he knows to be false.
sentences.zh: “真正的爱国者更不会散布明知错误的观点。

sentences.en: No man, who loves his country, fills the nation with clamorous complaints, that the protestant religion is in danger, because ‘popery is established in the extensive province of Quebec,’ a falsehood so open and shameless, that it can need no confutation among those who know that of which it is almost impossible for the most unenlightened zealot to be ignorant.”
sentences.zh: 没有一个热爱祖国的人会向全国大声抱怨，说新教正处于危险之中，因为‘教皇制度是在魁北克广大省份建立的’，这是一个如此公开和无耻的谎言，以至于那些知道这一点的人不需要反驳，即使是最无知的狂热分子也几乎不可能不知道这一点。”

blocks.html_en: Finally, in his closing peroration, Johnson urged the next House of Commons to “unite in a general abhorrence of those, who, by deceiving the credulous with fictitious mischiefs, overbearing the weak by audacity of falsehood, by appealing to the judgment of ignorance, and flattering the vanity of meanness … arrogate to themselves the name of patriots.”

sentences.en: Finally, in his closing peroration, Johnson urged the next House of Commons to “unite in a general abhorrence of those, who, by deceiving the credulous with fictitious mischiefs, overbearing the weak by audacity of falsehood, by appealing to the judgment of ignorance, and flattering the vanity of meanness …
sentences.zh: 最后，约翰逊在闭幕词中敦促下一届下议院“团结起来，共同憎恶那些以虚构的恶作剧欺骗轻信者、以无耻的谎言压倒弱者、诉诸无知的判断、迎合卑鄙的虚荣心的人……

sentences.en: arrogate to themselves the name of patriots.”
sentences.zh: 自诩爱国者之名。”

blocks.html_en: As we watch (or avoid watching) Donald Trump trying to turn the celebration of the United States’ 250th birthday into a celebration of Donald Trump, we would do well to remember Dr. Johnson’s thoughts. In wondering what he might think of the president’s ideas and actions this week, there is very little mystery. Let’s review a couple of those actions, as reported by Politico Playbook Friday morning:

sentences.en: As we watch (or avoid watching) Donald Trump trying to turn the celebration of the United States’ 250th birthday into a celebration of Donald Trump, we would do well to remember Dr. Johnson’s thoughts.
sentences.zh: 当我们看到（或避免看到）唐纳德·特朗普试图将美国250岁生日的庆祝活动变成唐纳德·特朗普的庆祝活动时，我们最好记住约翰逊博士的想法。

sentences.en: In wondering what he might think of the president’s ideas and actions this week, there is very little mystery.
sentences.zh: 想知道他对总统本周的想法和行动有何看法，这并没有什么神秘之处。

sentences.en: Let’s review a couple of those actions, as reported by Politico Playbook Friday morning:
sentences.zh: 让我们回顾一下 Politico Playbook 周五上午报道的其中一些行动：

blocks.html_en: • You saw that ridiculous video of Trump “talking” with the AI Teddy Roosevelt? Well, this was meant to be part of a “living museum recreating Theodore Roosevelt’s frontier experience,” as envisioned in a “planning document” from America250, a bipartisan, congressionally chartered, decade-old plan to launch various commemorations. From Playbook: “It hoped to draw 250,000 visitors for a nationally televised celebration on July 1 featuring A-list performers, immersive historical programming, a drone spectacular and, ultimately, the Theodore Roosevelt Presidential Library’s grand opening.” Instead, it launched with a visit from Trump.

sentences.en: You saw that ridiculous video of Trump “talking” with the AI Teddy Roosevelt?
sentences.zh: 你看过特朗普与人工智能泰迪·罗斯福“交谈”的可笑视频吗？

sentences.en: Well, this was meant to be part of a “living museum recreating Theodore Roosevelt’s frontier experience,” as envisioned in a “planning document” from America250, a bipartisan, congressionally chartered, decade-old plan to launch various commemorations.
sentences.zh: 好吧，这本来是“重现西奥多·罗斯福边疆经历的活生生博物馆”的一部分，正如《美国250》的一份“规划文件”中所设想的那样，这是一项两党、国会特许、已有十年历史的计划，旨在发起各种纪念活动。

sentences.en: From Playbook: “It hoped to draw 250,000 visitors for a nationally televised celebration on July 1 featuring A-list performers, immersive historical programming, a drone spectacular and, ultimately, the Theodore Roosevelt Presidential Library’s grand opening.”

blocks.html_en: But these, of course, are minor matters that will pass. The real hallmarks of Trump’s false patriotism are the things that make his tenure such a horrific embarrassment and civic tragedy to so many millions of Americans. The constant lies meant to glorify him and his reign. The toxic hatred of so many of the people he was elected to serve. The petty and immoral pursuit of his political enemies. The operatic and open corruption.

sentences.en: But these, of course, are minor matters that will pass.
sentences.zh: 但当然，这些都是小事，都会过去的。

sentences.en: The real hallmarks of Trump’s false patriotism are the things that make his tenure such a horrific embarrassment and civic tragedy to so many millions of Americans.
sentences.zh: 特朗普虚假爱国主义的真正标志是，他的任期对数百万美国人来说是一种可怕的尴尬和公民悲剧。

sentences.en: The constant lies meant to glorify him and his reign.
sentences.zh: 不断的谎言是为了荣耀他和他的统治。

sentences.en: The toxic hatred of so many of the people he was elected to serve.
sentences.zh: 他当选为众多人民的有毒仇恨。

sentences.en: The petty and immoral pursuit of his political enemies.
sentences.zh: 他的政治敌人的狭隘和不道德的追求。

sentences.en: The operatic and open corruption.
sentences.zh: 歌剧和公开腐败。

blocks.html_en: These are venal acts. But as July 4 approaches, it behooves us to remember specifically that they are also unpatriotic. Or worse: They are aggressively anti-patriotic. Real patriotism is truthful and humble; it tolerates and even welcomes dissent, and, understanding that the people rule in a democracy, it serves supporters and detractors equally; it seeks justice rather than revenge; and it understands that to pursue profit from office is abhorrent.

sentences.en: These are venal acts.
sentences.zh: 这些都是唯利是图的行为。

sentences.en: But as July 4 approaches, it behooves us to remember specifically that they are also unpatriotic.
sentences.zh: 但随着 7 月 4 日的临近，我们有必要特别记住，他们也是不爱国的。

sentences.en: Or worse: They are aggressively anti-patriotic.
sentences.zh: 或者更糟的是：他们非常反爱国。

sentences.en: Real patriotism is truthful and humble; it tolerates and even welcomes dissent, and, understanding that the people rule in a democracy, it serves supporters and detractors equally; it seeks justice rather than revenge; and it understands that to pursue profit from office is abhorrent.
sentences.zh: 真正的爱国是真诚的、谦虚的；它容忍甚至欢迎异议，并且理解人民在民主中统治，它平等地为支持者和反对者服务；它寻求正义而不是复仇；它明白从公职中谋取利益是令人憎恶的。

blocks.html_en: Trump knows none of that. He is a treacherous, know-nothing anti-patriot. The image that sticks with me, the photo that made me both roll my eyes and gasp in horror when I first saw it, was the one of Trump kissing an American flag. What a grotesque act of civic idolatry; in fact, let’s throw “idolatrous” in there too. And if you don’t understand why kissing a flag is an act of grotesque civic idolatry, then you, my friend, are part of the problem.

sentences.en: Trump knows none of that.
sentences.zh: 特朗普对此一无所知。

sentences.en: He is a treacherous, know-nothing anti-patriot.
sentences.zh: 他是一个背信弃义、一无所知的反爱国者。

sentences.en: The image that sticks with me, the photo that made me both roll my eyes and gasp in horror when I first saw it, was the one of Trump kissing an American flag.
sentences.zh: 这张让我印象深刻的照片，当我第一次看到这张照片时，它让我翻白眼，惊恐地喘着粗气，那就是特朗普亲吻美国国旗的照片。

sentences.en: What a grotesque act of civic idolatry; in fact, let’s throw “idolatrous” in there too.
sentences.zh: 这是多么荒唐的公民偶像崇拜行为啊！事实上，我们也把“偶像崇拜”也放进去吧。

sentences.en: And if you don’t understand why kissing a flag is an act of grotesque civic idolatry, then you, my friend, are part of the problem.
sentences.zh: 如果你不明白为什么亲吻国旗是一种怪诞的公民偶像崇拜行为，那么你，我的朋友，就是问题的一部分。

blocks.html_en: Let’s close with a few more thoughts on patriotism from some people who actually knew what it means:

sentences.en: Let’s close with a few more thoughts on patriotism from some people who actually knew what it means:
sentences.zh: 最后，让我们听听一些真正了解爱国主义含义的人对爱国主义的更多想法：

blocks.html_en: George Washington: “Guard against the impostures of pretended patriotism.”

sentences.en: George Washington: “Guard against the impostures of pretended patriotism.”
sentences.zh: 乔治·华盛顿：“谨防假装爱国主义的骗局。”

blocks.html_en: G.K. Chesterton: “‘My country, right or wrong’ is a thing no patriot would ever think of saying except in a desperate case. It is like saying ‘My mother, drunk or sober.’”

sentences.en: G.K.
Chesterton: “‘My country, right or wrong’ is a thing no patriot would ever think of saying except in a desperate case.
sentences.zh: 切斯特顿：“除非在绝望的情况下，任何爱国者都不会想到说‘我的国家，无论对错’。

sentences.en: It is like saying ‘My mother, drunk or sober.’”
sentences.zh: 这就像说‘我的母亲，无论醉酒还是清醒。’”

blocks.html_en: Albert Einstein: “Heroism on command, senseless violence, and all the loathsome nonsense that goes by the name of patriotism—how passionately I hate them!”

sentences.en: Albert Einstein: “Heroism on command, senseless violence, and all the loathsome nonsense that goes by the name of patriotism—how passionately I hate them!”
sentences.zh: 阿尔伯特·爱因斯坦：“指挥下的英雄主义、毫无意义的暴力，以及所有打着爱国主义旗号的令人厌恶的胡言乱语——我是多么讨厌他们！”

blocks.html_en: And maybe my favorite, from Clarence Darrow: “True patriotism hates injustice in its own land more than anywhere else.”

sentences.en: And maybe my favorite, from Clarence Darrow: “True patriotism hates injustice in its own land more than anywhere else.”
sentences.zh: 也许我最喜欢克拉伦斯·达罗的一句话：“真正的爱国主义比其他任何地方都更痛恨自己土地上的不公正现象。”

blocks.html_en: There is still much to celebrate about the United States of America—its art and literature and music, its scientific achievements, its physical beauty, and of course the principles of liberty it introduced to the world 250 years ago and toward which we daily and yearly strive. The anti-patriots do have the upper hand right now, but more and more people are seeing through them. In addition, they are also making real, Johnson- and Darrow-esque patriots of millions who were once disengaged. That is something to be hopeful about, and to celebrate, this weekend.

sentences.en: There is still much to celebrate about the United States of America—its art and literature and music, its scientific achievements, its physical beauty, and of course the principles of liberty it introduced to the world 250 years ago and toward which we daily and yearly strive.
sentences.zh: 美利坚合众国仍有很多值得庆祝的地方——它的艺术、文学和音乐、它的科学成就、它的自然之美，当然还有它 250 年前向世界介绍的自由原则，也是我们日复一日、年复一年为之奋斗的自由原则。

sentences.en: The anti-patriots do have the upper hand right now, but more and more people are seeing through them.
sentences.zh: 反爱国分子目前确实占据了上风，但越来越多的人看穿了他们。

sentences.en: In addition, they are also making real, Johnson- and Darrow-esque patriots of millions who were once disengaged.
sentences.zh: 此外，他们还让数百万曾经脱离接触的人成为真正的约翰逊和达罗式的爱国者。

sentences.en: That is something to be hopeful about, and to celebrate, this weekend.
sentences.zh: 这是本周末值得期待和庆祝的事情。

blocks.html_en: Was Thursday among the darkest days in the history of the Supreme Court? You could make a case. First, a majority cleared the way for a pesticide manufacturer to get thousands of lawsuits off its books from farmers who’d used its product and gotten cancer. Next, it ruled that the administration could turn away asylum-seekers at the border. And then it held that gun owners could now freely carry their weapons into private establishments that serve the public.

sentences.en: Was Thursday among the darkest days in the history of the Supreme Court?
sentences.zh: 周四是最高法院历史上最黑暗的日子之一吗？

sentences.en: You could make a case.
sentences.zh: 你可以举个例子。

sentences.en: First, a majority cleared the way for a pesticide manufacturer to get thousands of lawsuits off its books from farmers who’d used its product and gotten cancer.
sentences.zh: 首先，多数票为一家农药制造商扫清了道路，使其能够摆脱因使用其产品而患癌症的农民提起的数千起诉讼。

sentences.en: Next, it ruled that the administration could turn away asylum-seekers at the border.
sentences.zh: 接下来，它裁定政府可以在边境拒绝寻求庇护者。

sentences.en: And then it held that gun owners could now freely carry their weapons into private establishments that serve the public.
sentences.zh: 然后它认为枪支拥有者现在可以自由地将武器携带到为公众服务的私人场所。

blocks.html_en: Let’s pause over that one for a paragraph. Here’s a good description of the particulars of the gun case and the legal arguments on both sides. But the upshot is this: Everywhere in America, gun owners will presumably be able to take their guns to shops, stores, malls, movie theaters, restaurants, bars, amusement parks, Baby Gaps, you name it. Does any rational person think that the Founders, who simply wanted men to have muskets to protect themselves from invaders, would want someone to be able to take a military-style semiautomatic rifle and 600 rounds of ammo into a Chuck E. Cheese?

blocks.html_en: But the worst of Thursday’s big four decisions was Mullin v. Doe, which will allow the Trump administration to begin deporting Haitians and Syrians who were granted Temporary Protected Status by the Obama administration in 2010 and 2012, respectively. My colleague Matt Ford shredded the decision in his piece, writing that the court “effectively blessed Trump’s bigotry toward Haitians and dealt potentially catastrophic damage to federal civil rights laws.”

sentences.en: But the worst of Thursday’s big four decisions was Mullin v.
sentences.zh: 但周四四大判决中最糟糕的是穆林诉。

sentences.en: Doe, which will allow the Trump administration to begin deporting Haitians and Syrians who were granted Temporary Protected Status by the Obama administration in 2010 and 2012, respectively.
sentences.zh: 美国能源部，这将允许特朗普政府开始驱逐分别于 2010 年和 2012 年获得奥巴马政府临时保护身份的海地人和叙利亚人。

sentences.en: My colleague Matt Ford shredded the decision in his piece, writing that the court “effectively blessed Trump’s bigotry toward Haitians and dealt potentially catastrophic damage to federal civil rights laws.”
sentences.zh: 我的同事马特·福特在他的文章中撕碎了这一裁决，写道法院“实际上支持了特朗普对海地人的偏见，并对联邦民权法造成了潜在的灾难性损害。”

blocks.html_en: The cases combine to give the executive branch more power. They turn several lower court decisions on their head (as The New York Times notes today, immigration hard-liners had lost case after case on TPS until yesterday). And in the case of Mullin, in particular, the highest legal authority in the land—namely, Samuel Alito, writing for the majority—pretends that Donald Trump’s blatant racism toward Haitians doesn’t exist; that there was nothing “overtly racial” in Trump’s many disgusting and false comments about the Haitian community of Springfield, Ohio, and beyond.

sentences.en: The cases combine to give the executive branch more power.

sentences.en: And in the case of Mullin, in particular, the highest legal authority in the land—namely, Samuel Alito, writing for the majority—pretends that Donald Trump’s blatant racism toward Haitians doesn’t exist; that there was nothing “overtly racial” in Trump’s many disgusting and false comments about the Haitian community of Springfield, Ohio, and beyond.
sentences.zh: 特别是在穆林的案件中，该国最高法律权威——即为大多数人撰稿的塞缪尔·阿利托——假装唐纳德·特朗普对海地人的公然种族主义并不存在；特朗普对俄亥俄州斯普林菲尔德及其他地区海地社区的许多令人厌恶和虚假的评论中没有任何“明显的种族歧视”。

blocks.html_en: This conservative court is out of control—blatantly partisan and ideological, the six-member majority scarcely even pretends otherwise anymore.

sentences.en: This conservative court is out of control—blatantly partisan and ideological, the six-member majority scarcely even pretends otherwise anymore.
sentences.zh: 这个保守的法院已经失控了——公然的党派和意识形态，六名成员中的多数几乎不再假装不是这样。

blocks.html_en: Some major decisions about executive power—Trump’s power—are yet to be handed down this term, involving the firing of Fed Governor Lisa Cook, the removal of Democratic appointees from independent agencies, and of course the birthright citizenship case. If the court rules predictably on two of these three, or certainly on all three, it will have completed a term—with the aforementioned four decisions already on the books, as well as Callais v. Louisiana, which did away with Section 2 of the Voting Rights Act—that might well be the most reactionary in its history. And all this is on top of the earlier reversal of a 49-year-old precedent in 1973’s Roe v. Wade and the handing to Trump of sweeping immunity for all “official” acts.

sentences.zh: 一些有关行政权力（特朗普的权力）的重大决定尚未在本任期内下达，包括解雇美联储理事丽莎·库克（Lisa Cook）、罢免独立机构中的民主党任命人员，当然还有与生俱来的公民权案件。

blocks.html_en: It’s now unavoidable: This has to be a front-and-center issue in 2028. Democratic presidential contenders will have to answer the question: What do you plan to do about the Supreme Court?

blocks.html_en: Many of them will be afraid to dip a foot into these waters. They shouldn’t be. Poll after poll shows us that majorities disapprove of the court and think of its decisions as being more political than jurisprudential. According to Gallup, disapproval of the court topped 50 percent five years ago and has stayed there ever since (in contrast, that number was just 29 percent as recently as 2010). So the public—not just the progressive base of the party—is ready to hear ideas.

sentences.en: Many of them will be afraid to dip a foot into these waters.
sentences.zh: 他们中的许多人会害怕将脚浸入这些水域。

sentences.en: They shouldn’t be.
sentences.zh: 他们不应该这样。

sentences.en: Poll after poll shows us that majorities disapprove of the court and think of its decisions as being more political than jurisprudential.
sentences.zh: 一次又一次的民意调查显示，大多数人不赞成法院，并认为其判决更多的是政治性而非法理性。

sentences.en: According to Gallup, disapproval of the court topped 50 percent five years ago and has stayed there ever since (in contrast, that number was just 29 percent as recently as 2010).
sentences.zh: 根据盖洛普的数据，五年前，对法院的反对率高达 50%，此后一直保持这一水平（相比之下，2010 年这一数字仅为 29%）。

sentences.en: So the public—not just the progressive base of the party—is ready to hear ideas.
sentences.zh: 因此，公众——不仅仅是党内的进步派——已经准备好听取想法。

blocks.html_en: Terms limits, the most common idea bruited, are fine. But imposing term limits won’t really change the makeup of the court for years; maybe decades. How many more rights will they strip away before then? How much more power will they give to the uber-rich to buy political campaigns and candidates? How much more immunity will they grant to corporations? How many new ways will they find to weaken protections for workers and litigants against corporate power? And perhaps most of all, how will they figure out how to allow the executive branch to undermine the laws passed by Congress and refuse to write regulations and enforce the laws Congress has passed?

sentences.en: Terms limits, the most common idea bruited, are fine.
sentences.zh: 术语限制是最常见的想法，但没有问题。

sentences.en: But imposing term limits won’t really change the makeup of the court for years; maybe decades.
sentences.zh: 但多年来，施加任期限制并不会真正改变法院的构成。也许几十年。

sentences.en: How many more rights will they strip away before then?
sentences.zh: 在此之前他们还会剥夺多少权利？

sentences.en: How much more power will they give to the uber-rich to buy political campaigns and candidates?
sentences.zh: 他们将赋予超级富豪多少权力来收买政治竞选活动和候选人？

sentences.en: How much more immunity will they grant to corporations?
sentences.zh: 他们将给予企业多少额外的豁免权？

sentences.en: How many new ways will they find to weaken protections for workers and litigants against corporate power?
sentences.zh: 他们会找到多少新方法来削弱对工人和诉讼当事人免受企业权力影响的保护？

sentences.en: And perhaps most of all, how will they figure out how to allow the executive branch to undermine the laws passed by Congress and refuse to write regulations and enforce the laws Congress has passed?
sentences.zh: 也许最重要的是，他们将如何弄清楚如何允许行政部门破坏国会通过的法律并拒绝制定法规和执行国会通过的法律？

blocks.html_en: No—terms limits are no longer enough. It’s time to talk seriously about court expansion. And I think there’s a smart and totally constitutionally defensible way to do it.

sentences.en: No—terms limits are no longer enough.
sentences.zh: 不——任期限制已经不够了。

sentences.en: It’s time to talk seriously about court expansion.
sentences.zh: 是时候认真讨论法院扩建问题了。

sentences.en: And I think there’s a smart and totally constitutionally defensible way to do it.
sentences.zh: 我认为有一种明智且完全符合宪法的方法可以做到这一点。

blocks.html_en: The United States has 13 federal circuit courts. That number, naturally, grew over the course of the country’s history, as the number of states grew and as the population expanded. This is relevant here because each Supreme Court justice is responsible for overseeing a certain number of circuits. Historically, Congress has expanded the number of justices as it simultaneously increased the number of circuits.

sentences.en: The United States has 13 federal circuit courts.
sentences.zh: 美国有 13 个联邦巡回法院。

sentences.en: That number, naturally, grew over the course of the country’s history, as the number of states grew and as the population expanded.
sentences.zh: 随着国家数量的增加和人口的增加，这个数字自然会随着国家的历史进程而增长。

sentences.en: This is relevant here because each Supreme Court justice is responsible for overseeing a certain number of circuits.
sentences.zh: 这在这里是相关的，因为每个最高法院法官都负责监督一定数量的巡回法院。

sentences.en: Historically, Congress has expanded the number of justices as it simultaneously increased the number of circuits.
sentences.zh: 从历史上看，国会在增加巡回法院数量的同时也增加了大法官的数量。

blocks.html_en: Admittedly, all this happened a very long time ago. But still, it’s precedent. The court was established in 1789 at six justices. In 1807, Congress expanded the number of federal circuits to seven, and added a justice to match. In 1837, Congress created nine circuits and nine justices. In 1863—even while the United States of America had lost the 11 states of the Confederacy—Congress created 10 circuits and 10 justices. The current nine-justice format was set in 1869.

sentences.en: Admittedly, all this happened a very long time ago.
sentences.zh: 诚然，这一切都发生在很久以前了。

sentences.en: But still, it’s precedent.
sentences.zh: 但这仍然是先例。

sentences.en: The court was established in 1789 at six justices.
sentences.zh: 该法院成立于 1789 年，有六名法官。

sentences.en: In 1807, Congress expanded the number of federal circuits to seven, and added a justice to match.
sentences.zh: 1807 年，国会将联邦巡回法院的数量扩大到七个，并增加了一名与之相匹配的法官。

sentences.en: In 1837, Congress created nine circuits and nine justices.
sentences.zh: 1837 年，国会设立了九个巡回法院和九名法官。

sentences.en: In 1863—even while the United States of America had lost the 11 states of the Confederacy—Congress created 10 circuits and 10 justices.
sentences.zh: 1863 年，即使美利坚合众国失去了邦联的 11 个州，国会仍设立了 10 个巡回法院和 10 名法官。

sentences.en: The current nine-justice format was set in 1869.
sentences.zh: 目前的九位法官制度是在 1869 年制定的。

blocks.html_en: Later expansions in the number of circuits did not simultaneously add justices. But why not revive that thought? The country has had today’s 13 circuits since 1982. The population of the country in 1982 was 230 million. Today, it’s around 345 million. That’s a lot more people. And the courts are horribly backlogged.

sentences.en: Later expansions in the number of circuits did not simultaneously add justices.
sentences.zh: 后来巡回法院数量的扩大并没有同时增加法官。

sentences.en: But why not revive that thought?
sentences.zh: 但为什么不重温这个想法呢？

sentences.en: The country has had today’s 13 circuits since 1982.
sentences.zh: 自 1982 年以来，该国目前已拥有 13 条赛道。

sentences.en: The population of the country in 1982 was 230 million.
sentences.zh: 1982年该国人口为2.3亿。

sentences.en: Today, it’s around 345 million.
sentences.zh: 如今，这一数字约为 3.45 亿。

sentences.en: That’s a lot more people.
sentences.zh: 还有很多人。

sentences.en: And the courts are horribly backlogged.
sentences.zh: 法院的案件积压严重。

blocks.html_en: That could be solved by just adding judges. But it’s also a justification for increasing the number of circuits. From there, a case can clearly be made that increasing the number of circuits requires increasing the number of high court justices. Or at the very least, Democrats can pursue a hybrid solution that would keep the number of circuits at 13 and add a large number of judges within those circuits—while increasing the size of the Supreme Court to 13. Democratic Congressman Hank Johnson of Georgia, a leader on these issues, introduced such a bill in 2023, and it had around 60 co-sponsors.

sentences.en: That could be solved by just adding judges.
sentences.zh: 只需增加法官就可以解决这个问题。

sentences.en: But it’s also a justification for increasing the number of circuits.
sentences.zh: 但这也是增加电路数量的理由。

sentences.en: From there, a case can clearly be made that increasing the number of circuits requires increasing the number of high court justices.
sentences.zh: 由此可见，增加巡回法院的数量需要增加高等法院法官的数量。

sentences.en: Or at the very least, Democrats can pursue a hybrid solution that would keep the number of circuits at 13 and add a large number of judges within those circuits—while increasing the size of the Supreme Court to 13.
sentences.zh: 或者至少，民主党可以寻求一种混合解决方案，将巡回法院的数量保持在 13 个，并在这些巡回法院中增加大量法官，同时将最高法院的规模增加到 13 个。

sentences.en: Democratic Congressman Hank Johnson of Georgia, a leader on these issues, introduced such a bill in 2023, and it had around 60 co-sponsors.
sentences.zh: 佐治亚州民主党众议员汉克·约翰逊是这些问题的领导者，他于 2023 年提出了这样一项法案，该法案有大约 60 个共同提案国。

blocks.html_en: It would all be completely constitutional and completely legal. Which is more than can be said for a lot of the things Trump and the Republicans are getting up to, as they try to find new and blatantly illegal ways to stop mail-in voting and otherwise take the franchise away from citizens.

sentences.en: It would all be completely constitutional and completely legal.
sentences.zh: 这一切都将完全符合宪法、完全合法。

sentences.en: Which is more than can be said for a lot of the things Trump and the Republicans are getting up to, as they try to find new and blatantly illegal ways to stop mail-in voting and otherwise take the franchise away from citizens.
sentences.zh: 对于特朗普和共和党正在做的许多事情来说，这是无法言说的，因为他们试图找到新的、公然非法的方式来阻止邮寄投票，并以其他方式剥夺公民的选举权。

blocks.html_en: But the big door-opener here by Trump and the GOP is their rancidly unconstitutional mid-decade redistricting move. The Constitution clearly and plainly states that districts will be redrawn every 10 years, after the decennial census. What Trump and his party are doing with this redistricting is completely lawless.

sentences.en: But the big door-opener here by Trump and the GOP is their rancidly unconstitutional mid-decade redistricting move.
sentences.zh: 但特朗普和共和党在这方面的重大举措是他们在十年中期采取的严重违宪的重新划分选区的举措。

sentences.en: The Constitution clearly and plainly states that districts will be redrawn every 10 years, after the decennial census.
sentences.zh: 宪法明确规定，在十年一次的人口普查之后，每十年重新划分选区。

sentences.en: What Trump and his party are doing with this redistricting is completely lawless.
sentences.zh: 特朗普及其政党的选区重新划定完全是无法无天的。

blocks.html_en: Once they’ve done that, all bets are off. Democrats should do whatever they need to do to rebalance power. But—they should stay within the law. What I’m talking about here, what Johnson’s bill would accomplish, would be entirely within the law. Congress can set the size of the Supreme Court. And I believe that a smart Democrat, framing the argument the right way, can take that case to the American people and win it. He or she can convince the voters that far from destroying the court, such an action would constitute saving it from its own extremism—and saving the rights we cherish that these ideologues are stripping away.

sentences.en: Once they’ve done that, all bets are off.
sentences.zh: 一旦他们这样做了，所有的赌注都消失了。

sentences.en: Democrats should do whatever they need to do to rebalance power.
sentences.zh: 民主党人应该采取一切必要措施来重新平衡权力。

sentences.en: But—they should stay within the law.
sentences.zh: 但是——他们应该遵守法律。

sentences.en: What I’m talking about here, what Johnson’s bill would accomplish, would be entirely within the law.
sentences.zh: 我在这里所说的，约翰逊法案将要实现的目标，将完全在法律范围内。

sentences.en: Congress can set the size of the Supreme Court.
sentences.zh: 国会可以设定最高法院的规模。

sentences.en: And I believe that a smart Democrat, framing the argument the right way, can take that case to the American people and win it.
sentences.zh: 我相信，一个聪明的民主党人，以正确的方式阐述论点，可以将这个案子带给美国人民并赢得胜利。

sentences.en: He or she can convince the voters that far from destroying the court, such an action would constitute saving it from its own extremism—and saving the rights we cherish that these ideologues are stripping away.
sentences.zh: 他或她可以让选民相信，这样的行动远非摧毁法院，而是将其从自身的极端主义中拯救出来，并拯救我们所珍视的、被这些空想家剥夺的权利。

blocks.html_en: This article first appeared in Fighting Words, a weekly TNR newsletter authored by editor Michael Tomasky. Sign up here.

sentences.en: This article first appeared in Fighting Words, a weekly TNR newsletter authored by editor Michael Tomasky.
sentences.zh: 本文首次发表于《Fighting Words》，这是由编辑迈克尔·托马斯基 (Michael Tomasky) 撰写的 TNR 每周通讯。

sentences.en: Sign up here.
sentences.zh: 在这里注册。

blocks.html_en: Latest From Politics

sentences.en: Latest From Politics
sentences.zh: 最新政治

blocks.html_en: When I read history, I often wonder what it must have felt like to live those events in real time, as I’m sure you do. Did it seem an ominous moment in June 1914 when Gavrilo Princip shot Archduke Franz-Ferdinand? If I were a Briton in September 1938, would I have had an uneasy sense of foreboding watching the newsreel of Neville Chamberlain stepping off that plane from Munich?

sentences.en: When I read history, I often wonder what it must have felt like to live those events in real time, as I’m sure you do.
sentences.zh: 当我阅读历史时，我经常想知道实时生活这些事件会是什么感觉，我相信你也会这样做。

sentences.en: Did it seem an ominous moment in June 1914 when Gavrilo Princip shot Archduke Franz-Ferdinand?
sentences.zh: 1914 年 6 月，加夫里洛·普林西普 (Gavrilo Princip) 枪杀弗朗茨·费迪南大公 (Archduke Franz-Ferdinand)，这是否是一个不祥的时刻？

sentences.en: If I were a Briton in September 1938, would I have had an uneasy sense of foreboding watching the newsreel of Neville Chamberlain stepping off that plane from Munich?
sentences.zh: 如果我是 1938 年 9 月的英国人，当我看到内维尔·张伯伦 (Neville Chamberlain) 从慕尼黑走下飞机的新闻片时，我是否会产生一种不安的预感？

blocks.html_en: I think such thoughts this week because I have no doubt that future historians and readers of history will surely wonder what the ever-living fuck we were all thinking when Donald Trump both started and then lost his immoral and pointless war with Iran. What we have just witnessed is almost beyond belief, and would be beyond belief if we didn’t all know going in that Trump is such an aggressively and willfully stupid human being, utterly impervious to knowledge and facts, serenely cocooned in his carapace of ignorance, surrounded by flatterers who patronize him as one does a child and who scream at Americans about his nonexistent genius, courage, and virility. They exist in a fantasyland.

sentences.en: I think such thoughts this week because I have no doubt that future historians and readers of history will surely wonder what the ever-living fuck we were all thinking when Donald Trump both started and then lost his immoral and pointless war with Iran.
sentences.zh: 我本周有这样的想法，因为我毫不怀疑，未来的历史学家和历史读者肯定会想知道，当唐纳德·特朗普开始并随后输掉与伊朗的不道德和毫无意义的战争时，我们都在想什么。

sentences.en: What we have just witnessed is almost beyond belief, and would be beyond belief if we didn’t all know going in that Trump is such an aggressively and willfully stupid human being, utterly impervious to knowledge and facts, serenely cocooned in his carapace of ignorance, surrounded by flatterers who patronize him as one does a child and who scream at Americans about his nonexistent genius, courage, and virility.
sentences.zh: 我们刚刚目睹的一切几乎令人难以置信，如果我们不知道特朗普是一个如此咄咄逼人、故意愚蠢的人，对知识和事实完全无动于衷，平静地包裹在无知的甲壳里，周围都是阿谀奉承者，他们像对待孩子一样对他不存在的天才、勇气和阳刚之气向美国人大喊大叫，那就令人难以置信。

sentences.en: They exist in a fantasyland.
sentences.zh: 他们存在于幻想世界中。

blocks.html_en: But we live in the real world, and in the real world, this war was a disaster in every imaginable sense. Let’s tally up the damage:

sentences.en: But we live in the real world, and in the real world, this war was a disaster in every imaginable sense.
sentences.zh: 但我们生活在现实世界中，而在现实世界中，这场战争从各种意义上来说都是一场灾难。

sentences.en: Let’s tally up the damage:
sentences.zh: 我们来算一下伤害：

blocks.html_en: • First of all, there was already a diplomatic agreement with Iran that was working; the International Atomic Energy Agency reported repeatedly that Iran was abiding by the Obama-era JCPOA deal. There was no need for Donald Trump to do anything.

sentences.en: First of all, there was already a diplomatic agreement with Iran that was working; the International Atomic Energy Agency reported repeatedly that Iran was abiding by the Obama-era JCPOA deal.
sentences.zh: 首先，与伊朗已经达成了一项正在发挥作用的外交协议；国际原子能机构多次报告称，伊朗遵守奥巴马时代的联合全面行动计划协议。

sentences.en: There was no need for Donald Trump to do anything.
sentences.zh: 唐纳德·特朗普不需要做任何事情。

blocks.html_en: • But of course, the deal was the handiwork of Barack Obama—the Kenyan Marxist who made a few jokes at Trump’s expense at a dinner one time; so Trump tore it to pieces.

sentences.en: But of course, the deal was the handiwork of Barack Obama—the Kenyan Marxist who made a few jokes at Trump’s expense at a dinner one time; so Trump tore it to pieces.
sentences.zh: 当然，这笔交易是巴拉克·奥巴马 (Barack Obama) 的杰作，这位肯尼亚马克思主义者有一次在一次晚宴上拿特朗普开玩笑；所以特朗普把它撕成碎片。

blocks.html_en: • Almost instantly, Iran started enriching uranium at levels well above the 3.67 percent limit set by the JCPOA. And why not? Trump broke the agreement. Of course they started enriching uranium at high levels again.

sentences.en: Almost instantly, Iran started enriching uranium at levels well above the 3.67 percent limit set by the JCPOA.
sentences.zh: 伊朗几乎立即开始浓缩铀，其浓度远高于《联合全面行动计划》规定的 3.67% 的限制。

sentences.en: And why not?
sentences.zh: 为什么不呢？

sentences.en: Trump broke the agreement.
sentences.zh: 特朗普破坏了协议。

sentences.en: Of course they started enriching uranium at high levels again.
sentences.zh: 当然，他们再次开始高浓度浓缩铀。

blocks.html_en: • In other words: The fact that Iran once again became a nuclear threat is entirely Donald Trump’s fault.

sentences.en: In other words: The fact that Iran once again became a nuclear threat is entirely Donald Trump’s fault.
sentences.zh: 换句话说：伊朗再次成为核威胁完全是唐纳德·特朗普的错。

blocks.html_en: • With respect to its capability to build a nuclear weapon, Iran’s “breakout time,” in the preferred parlance of diplomacy, went from 12 months to seven days. That is not a typo.

sentences.en: With respect to its capability to build a nuclear weapon, Iran’s “breakout time,” in the preferred parlance of diplomacy, went from 12 months to seven days.
sentences.zh: 就其制造核武器的能力而言，伊朗的“突破时间”（用外交术语来说）从 12 个月缩短到了 7 天。

sentences.en: That is not a typo.
sentences.zh: 这不是一个错字。

blocks.html_en: • So Trump discovers this one day. Maybe Benjamin Netanyahu explained it to him. We’ll learn that whole story at some future point. In any case, out of nowhere on the last day of February, with no warning, no prep, no nothing, Trump starts a war. Within hours, we bomb a school, killing around 120 children.

sentences.en: So Trump discovers this one day.
sentences.zh: 所以特朗普有一天发现了这一点。

sentences.en: Maybe Benjamin Netanyahu explained it to him.
sentences.zh: 也许本杰明·内塔尼亚胡向他解释过这一点。

sentences.en: We’ll learn that whole story at some future point.
sentences.zh: 我们将在未来的某个时候了解整个故事。

sentences.en: In any case, out of nowhere on the last day of February, with no warning, no prep, no nothing, Trump starts a war.
sentences.zh: 无论如何，特朗普在二月的最后一天突然发动了一场战争，没有任何警告，没有任何准备，什么也没有。

sentences.en: Within hours, we bomb a school, killing around 120 children.
sentences.zh: 几个小时内，我们轰炸了一所学校，造成约 120 名儿童死亡。

blocks.html_en: • From a purely military standpoint, the war goes fine. We suffer few casualties, although we do kill 3,500 or more people. But Iran counters by doing the thing that every expert in the world knew Iran would do if it was ever attacked: It asserts its control over the Strait of Hormuz. If you want to come through, you have to pay to play. Up go the gas prices.

sentences.en: From a purely military standpoint, the war goes fine.
sentences.zh: 从纯粹的军事角度来看，战争进展顺利。

sentences.en: We suffer few casualties, although we do kill 3,500 or more people.
sentences.zh: 尽管我们确实杀死了 3,500 人或更多，但我们伤亡很少。

sentences.en: But Iran counters by doing the thing that every expert in the world knew Iran would do if it was ever attacked: It asserts its control over the Strait of Hormuz.
sentences.zh: 但伊朗却做出了反击，世界上每一位专家都知道，如果伊朗受到攻击，伊朗会这么做：它声称对霍尔木兹海峡拥有控制权。

sentences.en: If you want to come through, you have to pay to play.
sentences.zh: 如果你想通过，就必须付费才能玩。

sentences.en: Up go the gas prices.
sentences.zh: 汽油价格上涨。

blocks.html_en: • Now Trump is trapped. And he’s starting to get bored because the regime didn’t collapse in two weeks like he thought it would. He wants out. So he sends his corrupt son-in-law, in bed with the Saudis and in the middle of trying to humiliate Albania for no good reason, to sort things out.

sentences.en: Now Trump is trapped.
sentences.zh: 现在特朗普被困住了。

sentences.en: And he’s starting to get bored because the regime didn’t collapse in two weeks like he thought it would.
sentences.zh: 他开始感到无聊，因为这个政权并没有像他想象的那样在两周内崩溃。

sentences.en: He wants out.
sentences.zh: 他想出去。

sentences.en: So he sends his corrupt son-in-law, in bed with the Saudis and in the middle of trying to humiliate Albania for no good reason, to sort things out.
sentences.zh: 因此，他派出腐败的女婿，与沙特人同床共枕，无缘无故地试图羞辱阿尔巴尼亚，以解决问题。

blocks.html_en: • That brings us to this week: the outlines of a deal that looks for all the world like a complete surrender. The United States of America, for only the second time in its 237 years on this earth, has unambiguously lost a war.

sentences.en: That brings us to this week: the outlines of a deal that looks for all the world like a complete surrender.
sentences.zh: 这就是本周的重点：一项对全世界来说都像是彻底投降的协议的轮廓。

sentences.en: The United States of America, for only the second time in its 237 years on this earth, has unambiguously lost a war.
sentences.zh: 美利坚合众国在地球上存在 237 年以来，这是第二次毫无疑问地输掉了一场战争。

blocks.html_en: People can debate the above. I’m counting Vietnam, obviously; there’s no doubt about that one. I reckon the War of 1812 and the Korean War as stalemates. Under the Treaty of Ghent, the United States and Britain just agreed to go back to the way things were before the war, and with respect to Korea, the line was the 38th parallel pre-bellum and postbellum.

sentences.en: People can debate the above.
sentences.zh: 人们可以对以上内容进行辩论。

sentences.en: I’m counting Vietnam, obviously; there’s no doubt about that one.
sentences.zh: 显然，我指的是越南；这是毫无疑问的。

sentences.en: I reckon the War of 1812 and the Korean War as stalemates.
sentences.zh: 我认为 1812 年战争和朝鲜战争是僵局。

sentences.en: Under the Treaty of Ghent, the United States and Britain just agreed to go back to the way things were before the war, and with respect to Korea, the line was the 38th parallel pre-bellum and postbellum.
sentences.zh: 根据《根特条约》，美国和英国刚刚同意回到战前的状态，而对于朝鲜来说，这条线是战前和战后的北纬38度线。

blocks.html_en: Some will say Iraq was a loss, but I rate it, too, as kind of a draw. It sure wasn’t a win of the sort Dick Cheney and Paul Wolfowitz told us to expect. It cost many trillions of dollars and killed hundreds of thousands. To a mixed result: Today, Iraq is a democracy, of a sort, but it’s a long way from being free. But I perhaps charitably call it a draw because the U.S. did achieve the core stated aim: It deposed Saddam Hussein.

sentences.en: Some will say Iraq was a loss, but I rate it, too, as kind of a draw.
sentences.zh: 有人会说伊拉克是一场失败，但我也将其视为平局。

sentences.en: It sure wasn’t a win of the sort Dick Cheney and Paul Wolfowitz told us to expect.
sentences.zh: 这肯定不是迪克·切尼和保罗·沃尔福威茨告诉我们所期望的那种胜利。

sentences.en: It cost many trillions of dollars and killed hundreds of thousands.
sentences.zh: 它花费了数万亿美元并导致数十万人死亡。

sentences.en: To a mixed result: Today, Iraq is a democracy, of a sort, but it’s a long way from being free.
sentences.zh: 结果好坏参半：如今，伊拉克在某种程度上是一个民主国家，但距离自由还有很长的路要走。

sentences.en: But I perhaps charitably call it a draw because the U.S. did achieve the core stated aim: It deposed Saddam Hussein.
